conjecture_title: Open Problem (Extending Induced Arithmetic Removal to Higher Complexity and General Groups)
paper_title: Induced arithmetic removal: complexity 1 patterns over finite fields
summary: The open problem—extending the induced arithmetic removal lemma to higher complexity patterns and to general abelian groups (including Z)—remains unresolved. Fox, Tidor, and Zhao established the lemma for complexity 1 patterns over finite fields in 2019; the original paper was published in Israel Journal of Mathematics (2022). A 2024 follow-up by Gladkova (arXiv:2412.15170) refines the complexity 1/finite-field result by proving that complete removal (no non-generic exceptions) is achievable for partition-regular patterns of complexity 1, but this does not address either of the two main open directions: higher complexity patterns or general abelian groups.

since_posted.title: Induced arithmetic removal for partition-regular patterns of complexity 1
since_posted.claim: Proves that complete (exception-free) induced arithmetic removal holds for all partition-regular arithmetic patterns of complexity 1 over finite fields, refining the Fox–Tidor–Zhao result, but does not extend to higher complexity patterns or general abelian groups.

search_queries: induced arithmetic removal lemma higher complexity abelian groups Fox Tidor Zhao
induced arithmetic removal lemma integers Z general abelian groups 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: The paper appeared in Israel Journal of Mathematics 248 (2022), 1–38. The only identified post-2019 follow-up (Gladkova 2024) works within the same complexity 1 / finite-field setting and does not resolve either open direction. No paper found that extends induced arithmetic removal to complexity ≥ 2 or to Z or other infinite abelian groups.
